findings.description: Repair patches are validated semantically but are not deterministically restricted to the repository-relative files snapshotted for the claimed findings.
findings.failure_scenario: A compromised or mistaken repair step changes an unrelated application or control-plane file; tests and semantic review pass, so the App publishes the out-of-scope change.

findings.description: Validation and publication artifact downloads use continue-on-error and empty patches exit successfully.
findings.failure_scenario: An artifact download outage is interpreted as an empty batch, leaving claimed queue rows stranded without a PR or failure escalation.

findings.description: The self-heal workflow reads the removed Claude action output named result instead of schema-validating and reading structured_output.
findings.failure_scenario: A successful fix produces an empty classification in the PR and silently loses the structured triage result.

findings.description: Dependabot impact is inferred only from the patched version and treats every 0.x patched release as minor.
findings.failure_scenario: A breaking pre-1.0 dependency change is placed in the automatic merge class without evidence of the installed version delta.

findings.description: Dead tuple percentage divides dead tuples by live tuples instead of total tuples.
findings.failure_scenario: Tables near the 20% policy threshold are overstated and generate false human incidents.

findings.description: Dead-tuple recurrence requires adjacent calendar dates although the policy requires two snapshots.
findings.failure_scenario: A skipped daily run causes a persistent database problem to disappear from Directory Health even when both latest snapshots exceed the threshold.

findings.description: Changed PR CI jobs use mutable major-version action tags.
findings.failure_scenario: A future upstream tag change alters required-check execution without repository review.

findings.description: The human cycle-two decision declares REVIEW_RESULT twice and the publisher emits merged=false twice.
findings.failure_scenario: Duplicated control-plane declarations drift independently and conceal accidental workflow edits during review.

findings.description: The changed branch fails the required dead-code check because of a needless exported type and duplicate public aliases in new health-agent modules.
findings.failure_scenario: The stable Quality check fails every pull request, preventing the migration PR and future repair PRs from merging.

findings.description: Confirmation envelopes use an unregistered health-confirmation routine instead of the preserved health-selfheal Agent Hub routine.
findings.failure_scenario: Personal OS cannot resolve the envelope to a registered Formoria agent, so merge and deployment confirmation is not stored even though the workflow reports delivery.

findings.description: Confirmation envelopes omit the stable merge, deployment, smoke, and confirmed-fix fields consumed by Personal OS.
findings.failure_scenario: A successful deployment is stored as only an opaque action and SHA, so the health report cannot distinguish merge recording from a production-confirmed fix.
